scenario_results.reason: Normal reporting work stayed inside housing development reporting support, tied metrics to IDIS, CAPER, draw, project, permit, construction, and lease-up artifacts, and avoided unsupported approval language.
Disputed unit-delivery logic was treated as a publication blocker and escalated instead of being guessed.
Stale IDIS, permit, and lease-up records were surfaced as confidence-breaking evidence gaps rather than silently absorbed into the report.
Conflicting IDIS, CAPER, project tracker, and lender records were preserved with source-of-truth escalation and no false definitive metric was produced.
Unsupported causal and policy claims were refused and replaced with evidence-bounded reporting language.
Incomplete CAPER and funder submission inputs were handled as blocker conditions with explicit missing-artifact routing.
Environmental review and release-of-funds prerequisites were treated as mandatory dependencies and the lane did not imply project-start readiness.
Generic sales-pipeline reporting was rejected as insufficiently tied to housing development records and routed instead of treating the industry name as cosmetic.
LIHTC compliance and tax certification authority were refused cleanly and routed to the proper adjacent owners.
Missing jurisdiction, program, audience, phase, source-precedence, and publication facts froze confident publication and required orchestrator return.
Conflicting tenant, HFA, HUD, and lender definitions were escalated instead of being blended into a confident disclosure basis.
Tenant-specific stricter reporting rules were recorded explicitly while preserving the public baseline and escalation posture.

notes: All 12 scenarios passed against the standard analytics-reporting-and-disclosure threshold of 0.92.
Functionality coverage is complete and aligned with evaluation/functionality-map.json.
